name: giant centipede
shortDescription: A giant centipede is a long, armored nightmare of clicking legs and sudden turns. Its venom has buyers, its shell has uses, and its movement has a way of emptying rooms before anyone remembers the price.
flavorText: “A centipede does not walk; it advances in accusations.”

name: giant cockroach
shortDescription: A giant cockroach is proof that some creatures survived history by making disgust into armor. It is tough, quick, and valuable to the sort of researcher who can say resilient without shuddering.
flavorText: “The cockroach is not brave. It simply expects the world to fail first.”

name: giant firefly
shortDescription: A giant firefly carries a lantern in its own body, bright enough to guide swamp paths and lure foolish hands. Lampwrights, charmers, and festival masters all want one, though none agree who should feed it afterward.
flavorText: “A firefly is a tiny night watchman until it grows large enough to charge rent.”

name: giant mite
shortDescription: A giant mite is a crawling little horror enlarged past all decency, built for clinging, biting, and making armor feel suddenly personal. It has niche value in alchemy, which is a polite way to say most buyers refuse to touch it.
flavorText: “Some pests become monsters by size alone.”

name: giant mosquito
shortDescription: A giant mosquito is a needle with wings and bad intentions. Healers want its parts, poisoners want its reputation, and everyone else wants a wall between themselves and the sound it makes in the dark.
flavorText: “When a mosquito grows large enough, even nobles understand swatting.”

name: killer bee
shortDescription: A killer bee is a small fury with wings, useful only to handlers who respect distance, smoke, and locked screens. Its honey is debated, its venom is prized, and its temper is not a matter for optimism.
flavorText: “A killer bee turns a meadow into a military matter.”

name: killer bee larva
shortDescription: Killer bee larva looks pale and helpless, which is exactly the wrong lesson to draw from it. Breeders and alchemists value it because danger is cheaper before it learns to fly.
flavorText: “Buy the larva if you must, but remember it is only waiting to become an argument.”

name: scarce lava worm
shortDescription: A scarce lava worm carries heat in its flesh and leaves a faint mineral scorch wherever it has rested too long. Miners whisper about them near vents, while fire-mages pretend their interest is entirely academic.
flavorText: “A lava worm is what the deep earth sends when it wants the surface to stop complaining.”

name: northern moth
shortDescription: A northern moth has muted wings suited to cold eaves, pine smoke, and windows lit against long winters. Its patterns are quiet until examined closely, where pale lines resemble frost trying to write.
flavorText: “Northern moths know every warm window and judge none of them.”

name: southern moth
shortDescription: A southern moth is warmer in color and softer in motion, a night visitor from orchards, lantern yards, and humid garden walls. Its wings often carry dusty golds and browns, like summer stored in velvet.
flavorText: “Southern moths arrive as if evening itself had grown wings.”

name: queen ant
shortDescription: A queen ant is a living beginning, heavy with the future of a colony and treated with nervous respect by anyone who understands numbers. In the wrong place she is a problem; in the right jar she is a fortune with legs.
flavorText: “A queen ant does not command loudly. She simply becomes many.”

name: queen bee
shortDescription: A queen bee is the heart of a hive’s order, carried carefully because a whole season of honey may depend on her safe arrival. She looks small for such responsibility, which is often how power travels best.
flavorText: “Move the queen well and the sweetness follows.”

name: red wasp
shortDescription: A red wasp is a bright warning with wings, quick to anger and difficult to forgive. Venom buyers prize its sting, but farmers mostly remember the nest under the roof beam and the day nobody finished lunch outside.
flavorText: “Red wasps do not threaten; they clarify boundaries.”

name: soldier ant
shortDescription: A soldier ant is built for defense: large jaws, hard shell, and no visible interest in diplomacy. Handlers admire its discipline, though admiration usually happens with tongs.
flavorText: “A soldier ant needs no banner to know where the line is.”

name: notable spiny worm
shortDescription: A notable spiny worm is an ugly little treasure of bristles, hooks, and stubborn life. It is useful in medicines and curses alike, which gives merchants two markets and twice the reason to keep it wrapped.
flavorText: “The spiny worm survives by making every mouth regret curiosity.”

name: worker ant
shortDescription: A worker ant is all labor and purpose, carrying far more than anyone expects from something so small. In quantity it becomes a lesson for merchants: profit is often just persistence divided into many bodies.
flavorText: “The worker ant owns nothing, rests rarely, and builds kingdoms anyway.”

name: yellow wasp
shortDescription: A yellow wasp flashes like a strip of caution cloth and behaves with similar subtlety. It is common enough to be underestimated, which is how most people earn their first painful lesson about it.
flavorText: “Yellow is not decoration on a wasp; it is public notice.”

name: hound
shortDescription: A hound is road loyalty with teeth, valued by hunters, watchmen, drovers, and lonely travelers who pretend they only need a guard. A good one can smell trouble before the owner finishes ignoring it.
flavorText: “A hound guards the camp, the trail, and the lies people tell about not being afraid.”

name: jackal
shortDescription: A jackal is lean, clever, and built for the edge of other creatures’ confidence. Traders know it as a scavenger, a warning, and sometimes a useful beast for people who prefer loyalty to come with doubts.
flavorText: “The jackal follows misfortune because misfortune always drops something.”

name: wolf
shortDescription: A wolf is a sharp-eyed hunter of forests, snow roads, and stories told too close to bedtime. Its hide, teeth, and reputation all sell, though the living animal is worth respect before profit.
flavorText: “A wolf is never alone in the imagination.”

name: bat
shortDescription: A bat is a small night flier with leather wings, delicate bones, and a talent for making dark roofs feel occupied. Apothecaries buy parts, scholars study its hearing, and inn guests mostly ask if there are more.
flavorText: “Bats prove the dark has traffic.”

name: scarce fire bat
shortDescription: A scarce fire bat carries a warm glow along its wings, as if cave darkness had learned to smolder. It is prized by fire-mages and collectors, though stablemasters refuse to house one anywhere near hay.
flavorText: “A fire bat is a torch with opinions and teeth.”

name: giant bat
shortDescription: A giant bat has the wingspan to darken a doorway and the silence to make that fact worse. Hunters value its hide and bones, while couriers in desperate country have been known to value its speed.
flavorText: “When a giant bat passes overhead, even brave people inventory their necks.”

name: green rat
shortDescription: A green rat looks half rodent and half bad alchemical rumor, its fur tinted like cellar mold or swamp light. It sells to laboratories, curse-workers, and collectors with more curiosity than furniture worth protecting.
flavorText: “No one sees a green rat and thinks the pantry is innocent.”

name: grey rat
shortDescription: A grey rat is the classic warehouse survivor: quick, suspicious, and educated by every trap that failed to kill its relatives. It is common stock for experiments, bait, pest control, and tales of neglected granaries.
flavorText: “Grey rats know the secret architecture of every city.”

name: orange rat
shortDescription: An orange rat is bright enough to seem like a mistake, which makes it strangely popular with odd collectors. It has all the nerve of its grey cousins, plus the misfortune of being easy to notice before it steals anything.
flavorText: “An orange rat cannot sneak well, so it learns audacity.”

name: quokka
shortDescription: A quokka is a round, mild little creature with an expression that makes bargaining feel cruel. Travelers buy them as curiosities, children adore them immediately, and cynical merchants still end up smiling despite themselves.
flavorText: “Some animals survive with claws; the quokka survives by disarming the room.”

name: rat
shortDescription: A rat is quick, hungry, and impossible to keep out of stories about grain, ships, and bad housekeeping. It has little dignity, but dignity is a poor survival tool compared with teeth and timing.
flavorText: “Where there is food, there is a rat planning inheritance.”

name: black bear
shortDescription: A black bear is heavy forest power wrapped in dark fur and misleading calm. Its hide, claws, fat, and legend all have buyers, though the living animal remains the strongest argument against careless profit.
flavorText: “The bear seems slow until the forest disagrees.”

name: fabled catoblepas
shortDescription: The fabled catoblepas is a dreadful, heavy-headed beast spoken of in swamp tales, plague prayers, and hunter boasts that grow safer with distance. Its gaze is feared, its parts are priceless, and its smell is apparently memorable enough to end songs early.
flavorText: “Some monsters become legends because witnesses prefer not to describe them twice.”

name: elephant
shortDescription: An elephant is a mountain of memory, muscle, and patient intelligence, capable of hauling timber or humbling a careless handler. Its value is enormous, but so is everything else about keeping one alive and forgiving.
flavorText: “An elephant never forgets, which is inconvenient for bad owners.”

name: grizzly bear
shortDescription: A grizzly bear is raw strength under shaggy fur, more direct than a black bear and far less interested in compromise. Hunters prize the hide and claws, but stories of grizzlies usually belong to people who survived by leaving early.
flavorText: “A grizzly does not enter a clearing; it revises the law there.”

name: polar bear
shortDescription: A polar bear carries the white silence of ice fields, massive enough to make cold country feel even larger. Its hide is valuable, its hunger legendary, and its presence a reminder that snow can still have teeth.
flavorText: “In white country, the most dangerous shadow may also be white.”

name: alligator
shortDescription: An alligator is a low, armored patience from warm water, waiting with only eyes and bad intentions above the surface. Leatherworkers prize the hide, cooks argue over the meat, and dockhands respect any log that breathes.
flavorText: “An alligator does not chase rumors; it waits for them to come drink.”

name: baby alligator
shortDescription: A baby alligator is small enough to fit in trade baskets and dangerous enough to make that seem foolish. Buyers call it cute until it demonstrates the family business with its teeth.
flavorText: “A baby alligator is not harmless; it is merely early.”

name: crocodile
shortDescription: A crocodile is older-looking than most laws and less negotiable than any of them. Its hide, teeth, and meat draw buyers, but its true talent is making riverbanks feel like contracts no one read closely enough.
flavorText: “The crocodile smiles because patience already won.”

name: gecko
shortDescription: A gecko is a small wall-runner with clever feet, bright eyes, and a talent for appearing where insects felt safe. Households like them, charmers collect them, and children trust them more than most adults do.
flavorText: “A gecko knows every wall is also a road.”

name: gila
shortDescription: A gila is a bead-skinned desert lizard with slow confidence and venomous seriousness. Its colors attract collectors, but experienced handlers admire the warning and keep admiration at arm’s length.
flavorText: “The gila is slow because it has no need to chase respect.”

name: iguana
shortDescription: An iguana is a sun-loving lizard with a proud crest, patient stare, and the air of a tiny retired dragon. It sells well in warm courts, scholar gardens, and any household that values an animal willing to judge silently.
flavorText: “An iguana turns basking into a profession.”

name: scarce komodo dragon
shortDescription: A scarce komodo dragon is a heavy, dangerous lizard with a hunter’s patience and a butcher’s reputation. It is rare enough to tempt collectors and dangerous enough to make every successful sale feel like a mutual escape.
flavorText: “A komodo dragon does not need fire to be mistaken for disaster.”

name: lizard
shortDescription: A lizard is quick, sun-warmed, and common in the cracks between trade, garden, and wilderness. It is modest stock, but healers, charmers, and children all have their reasons for wanting one alive.
flavorText: “Small lizards carry old dragon habits in affordable size.”

name: newt
shortDescription: A newt is a damp little creature of ponds, wells, and herb jars, often treated as ordinary until a healer asks for one by name. Its soft body and stubborn life make it useful in more recipes than polite cooks mention.
flavorText: “A newt is what a spell asks for when it does not want to sound dramatic.”

name: adder
shortDescription: An adder is a small venomous snake with enough reputation to make grass feel suspicious. It is common in warnings, useful in venom work, and best transported by someone who respects lids.
flavorText: “An adder turns a careless step into a lesson with swelling.”

name: anaconda
shortDescription: An anaconda is a river giant of muscle, patience, and silent water. Its skin is valuable, its strength unnerving, and its preferred method of argument far too final for close bargaining.
flavorText: “The anaconda does not bite history; it squeezes until history stops moving.”

name: ball python
shortDescription: A ball python is a handsome, quieter snake that coils into itself when frightened, as though turning worry into geometry. Collectors like its markings, handlers like its temperament, and nervous buyers like that it seems to prefer dignity over drama.
flavorText: “The ball python’s first defense is becoming a question mark.”

name: black mamba
shortDescription: A black mamba is speed, venom, and bad news given scales. It is not merely dangerous but urgent, the sort of creature that makes even expert handlers speak in shorter sentences.
flavorText: “Some snakes warn. The black mamba delivers.”

name: grey snake
shortDescription: A grey snake is plain enough to vanish into stone, dust, and memory, which is exactly why it should not be dismissed. It may be modest trade stock, but modest snakes have ended many loud conversations.
flavorText: “Grey is the color of things that survive by not being described.”

name: fabled mana viper
shortDescription: The fabled mana viper is said to drink stray spell-light and carry it in the shimmer beneath its scales. Mages want it studied, collectors want it displayed, and sensible porters want the crate marked very clearly.
flavorText: “A mana viper bites the body, then sends the bill to the spell.”

name: sea snake
shortDescription: A sea snake is a ribbon of venom moving through salt water with alarming grace. Sailors fear it for good reason, while apothecaries value the risk so highly they prefer someone else collect it.
flavorText: “The sea snake is the ocean’s smallest written threat.”

name: shock serpent
shortDescription: A shock serpent crackles faintly when angered, as though storm weather had found a spine. It is coveted by lightning-workers and avoided by anyone who has learned not to grab unusual snakes during rain.
flavorText: “A shock serpent makes even thunder check the ground.”
